description: §60.2/§60.4 — a canonical valid `<api>` + `<request api=>`: a required base=, a well-formed endpoint (recognized method, path template whose `${id}` binds the reqShape UserQuery.id, a variant `: ResponseT`), and a `<request>` naming that endpoint with a matching args cell. The compile-shape proof that the whole `<api>` contract parses + type-checks with NO E-API-* / E-TYPE-UNKNOWN-NAME / W-API-RESPONSE-NOT-VARIANT firing, and codegen (the emitted thin typed fetch + parseVariant decode) is clean.
rationale: §60.2/§60.4: a well-formed `<api>` (base=, recognized method, bound path param, variant response) invoked by a shape-matching `<request api=>` is valid — none of the seven E-API-* codes, E-TYPE-UNKNOWN-NAME, the W-API-RESPONSE-NOT-VARIANT info-lint (the response is a variant), or E-CODEGEN-INVALID-LOGIC fire. §60.6 client-only: no server bundle is implied.
